commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Arachite; perhaps descended from the ancient Arachites, who dwelt near Arad and Tripoli, where the pretended Sabbatic river is said to flow; (Josephus, Jewish Wars xii. 13.) or rather, as the names are written in a different manner, this person might be a native of Arachi, in Benjamin, west of Bethel, Josue xvi. 2. (Calmet) See chap. xv. 32.
Friend. This was his peculiar title of office, 1 Paralipomenon xxvii. 33. (Calmet)
King. (Salve.) Literally, "Hail, O King "in both places. The salutation is repeated for greater emphasis. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
